Stacking weights for binary responses

stacking_binomial.Rd

Compute stacking weights for combining multiple external prediction models under a binomial likelihood. Weights are estimated by maximising the Bernoulli log-likelihood of the stacked prediction Yw against the binary response z, using optim. Predictions outside [eps, 1 - eps] are penalised with a large objective value to keep the optimisation in the interior of the unit interval.

Usage

stacking_binomial(
  Y,
  z,
  w_init = NULL,
  method = "BFGS",
  maxit = 1e+06,
  reltol = 1e-10,
  eps = 1e-06,
  big = 1e+20
)

Arguments

Y

An n x M numeric matrix of external predictions on the probability scale (each column in [0, 1]).

z

A numeric vector of binary responses (0/1) of length n.

w_init

Optional numeric vector of initial weights (length M). Defaults to equal weights rep(1/M, M).

method

Optimisation method passed to optim. Defaults to "BFGS".

maxit

Maximum number of iterations for optim().

reltol

Relative convergence tolerance for optim().

eps

Numerical tolerance: stacked predictions outside [eps, 1 - eps] are penalised with big.

big

Penalty value returned when the stacked prediction falls outside the valid range. Should be larger than any plausible negative log-likelihood.

Value

A list with the following elements:

weights

A numeric vector of length M of estimated stacking weights.

fitted

The stacked prediction Y %*% weights.

value

The minimised negative log-likelihood.

convergence

Convergence code from optim(); 0 indicates success.

message

Convergence message from optim().

optim

The full optim() return object.

See also

stacking_gaussian, stacking_poisson, calcExtY

On this page
